tone: clear, technical, complete, accessible to adjacent research areas
audience: research readers who may not know every project detail
goals: Preserve all technical meaning from the Chinese source.
Explain motivation and method clearly enough for a preprint audience.
Favor readability and complete context over extreme compression.
prefer: Define specialized terms when they first appear.
Use explicit transitions between motivation, method, and evidence.
Keep claims traceable to the source manuscript.
avoid: Do not overclaim novelty or generality.
Do not add missing experiments or citations as if they were in the source.
Do not remove limitations or uncertainty stated in the source.
sentenceRules: Break overly dense Chinese sentences into multiple English sentences when needed.
citationRules: Flag citation gaps only when necessary.
latexRules: Preserve LaTeX structure, labels, references, citations, figures, tables, and equations.
Escape special characters in normal prose.
Do not alter template or bibliography files unless explicitly requested.

abstract: Summarize the problem, approach, and main findings without excessive detail.
introduction: Provide enough background for cross-area readers.
Make the gap and contribution explicit.
method: Use stepwise descriptions for systems and algorithms.
experiment: Tie observations directly to metrics, baselines, or qualitative findings.
general: Prioritize clarity, fidelity, and complete context.

examplesPolicy: Use examples as style references only; do not copy external content.
